activities.markdown:
# 8th Note Hi-Hat Patterns

The most common rock beat uses 8th notes on the hi-hat.

Count: **1 + 2 + 3 + 4 +**

Hi-hat plays on every count (8 times per bar). Snare on 2 and 4. Kick on 1 and 3.

This is the foundation of thousands of songs. Master this pattern at multiple tempos before adding variations.

activities.title: Basic 8th Note Rock Beat
activities.description: The essential rock groove. Hi-hat on every 8th note, snare locks the backbeat on 2 and 4, kick drives beats 1 and 3. Start slow and build speed gradually.

activities.question: In a basic 8th note rock beat, how many hi-hat strokes are in one measure of 4/4?
activities.explanation: 8th notes in 4/4: 4 beats × 2 eighth notes each = 8 hi-hat strokes per measure (counted as 1 + 2 + 3 + 4 +).
